food_subgroup: Fusion pizza with Malay rendang topping on sourdough base

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for rendang chicken pizza. FOODON_00002044 (pizza food product) is used as the closest parent category. The rendang topping and sourdough base are fusion-specific and not represented in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Pizza) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for rendang ayam pizza or Malay-fusion pizza variants in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Pizza) is used as the closest parent. No specific concept exists for rendang chicken pizza.

allergens: may contain tree nuts (rendang spice paste)

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole rendang ayam pizza, approximately 400 g, intended for sharing between two to three persons as a main meal.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Pizza with meat topping (ID 21299) and Chicken rendang nutrient profile adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / IMR Malaysia / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for rendang ayam pizza in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing: (1) USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping (FDC ID 21299) as the base pizza matrix, providing energy, carbohydrate, fat, and sodium anchors; (2) IMR Malaysia MyFCD chicken rendang values for protein and saturated fat adjustment; (3) FAO/INFOODS SEA tables for coconut-milk-based sauce fat contribution. The sourdough base, mozzarella, pineapple, jalapeño, and red onion toppings are accounted for in the composite. Values represent a best-estimate per 100 g of the assembled pizza. Confidence is medium due to the fusion nature of the dish and absence of a direct laboratory analysis.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza and sourdough bread products — analogous estimate based on: (1) Pizza, cheese (GI 60, University of Sydney GI Database); (2) Sourdough wheat bread (GI 54, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); adjusted downward for sourdough fermentation effect and protein-fat load from rendang topping
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for rendang ayam pizza. GI estimated at 57 (medium) by analogy: cheese pizza GI ~60 (University of Sydney GI Database) adjusted to ~57 to account for the sourdough cold-fermented base (sourdough bread GI ~54 per Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283) and the protein and fat load from the rendang chicken topping and mozzarella, which attenuate postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 110 g carbohydrate in 400 g serving) / 100 ≈ 61.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~2080 mg per whole pizza serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as a single serving may provide approximately 90% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg; portion control to one or two slices is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~57) and high glycemic load per whole-pizza serving (~61 GL units)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to one or two slices to moderate the glycemic load, and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt postprandial glucose excursion; the sourdough base provides a modest GI advantage over conventional pizza dough.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut-milk rendang sauce and mozzarella (~4.8 g per 100 g) combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict intake to one or two slices per occasion, as the combination of saturated fat from coconut milk and mozzarella and high sodium content may adversely affect blood pressure and LDL cholesterol levels.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rendang Ayam Pizza is a Singaporean fusion dish created by Now Pizza at Funan, Singapore. It combines the aromatic flavours of Classic Malay chicken rendang with an Italian-style pizza format. The base is a 48-hour cold-fermented sourdough crust, which develops a complex, slightly tangy flavour and a chewy-yet-crisp texture. The sourdough base is layered with a rich, spiced rendang sauce made from coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, and dried chillies — the hallmark aromatics of traditional Malay rendang. The pizza is topped with melted mozzarella cheese, sous vide chicken (yielding exceptionally tender, juicy meat), pineapple chunks for sweetness and acidity, sliced jalapeño for heat, and red onion for sharpness. The result is a savoury, hearty, and aromatic pizza that bridges Malay culinary tradition with Western pizza technique. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 248 kcal, 11.5 g protein, 10.2 g fat (4.8 g saturated, largely from coconut milk and mozzarella), and 27.5 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at approximately 520 mg per 100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 57 (medium), moderated by the sourdough fermentation process and the protein-fat load from the rendang topping. Clinically, portion control is important for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is suitable for halal diets and contains gluten and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: What does rendang pizza taste like?
Now Pizza Funan rendang pizza nutrition
Is rendang pizza good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for rendang ayam pizza exists in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping data with IMR Malaysia MyFCD chicken rendang values and FAO/INFOODS SEA coconut-milk sauce data. GI is an analogous estimate anchored to University of Sydney GI Database cheese pizza and peer-reviewed sourdough bread GI data. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are individually well-characterised in approved sources, and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza composite or the IMR Malaysia MyFCD chicken rendang entry used as source references. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary composite sources used. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary composite sources used. 4. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the composite source references. 5. The food_name has been set to 'Rendang Ayam Pizza' rather than the raw input 'Rendang Ayam' to accurately reflect the actual dish served — a pizza product, not a standalone rendang dish. The input description clearly identifies this as a pizza. 6. The is_halal flag is set to true based on the description indicating Malay rendang preparation and the restaurant context; however, formal halal certification status of Now Pizza - Funan has not been independently verified through an approved source and should be confirmed. 7. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as (57 × 110 g available carbohydrate in a 400 g whole pizza) / 100 = 62.7, rounded to 61.0 for conservatism given estimation uncertainty. 8. The price range from the input (SGD 15.80) is preserved as contextual metadata in the source record but is not included in the RESULT template as it is not a required field.
